rationale: The assistant stayed at the requested local repair level and supplied another entry-page explanation. It did not identify the pattern of three prior information-overlay fixes, did not brake the fourth overlay request, and did not move upstream to the recurring service-confusion owner or propose subtraction, consolidation, or structural redesign.

The assistant directly adds another submission-page reminder and placement suggestion. It does not identify the repeated pattern of three prior downstream information overlays, does not brake the N+1 overlay request, and does not move upstream to the recurring confusion/failure owner or propose subtraction, consolidation, or structural redesign.

The response immediately triggers the brake, names the repeated pattern of adding more overlays across SMS/forms/signage, treats the parking SMS as only a one-time emergency patch rather than the default operating mode, and moves upstream to a consolidated arrival guide with a future distribution/check process. It still provides a temporary SMS, but it is explicitly bounded and paired with structural consolidation, so it avoids the fail signal of simply adding another reminder layer.

The response immediately brakes the fourth manual-gate request, names the pattern as repeated downstream human patching, references the prior复看岗/拦截岗/抽检班 plus the requested确认人, and moves upstream to recurring release, identification, data/field, and responsibility failures. It permits a one-day emergency confirmer, but explicitly blocks making it a standing gate and requires structural repair plus removal of temporary posts, so the fail signal of simply adding another manual checkpoint is not observed.

The assistant immediately triggers the brake, explicitly rejects making another entrance verifier a standing role, recognizes the repeated pattern of manual checks masking system/rule gaps, and moves upstream to rule/data/process fixes such as automatic numbering, format validation, duplicate checks, and pre-submit prompts. It allows only a bounded one-day emergency check with automatic removal, so it does not fall into the fail signal of simply adding another permanent manual gate.

The response wakes up immediately with a brake, refuses making the refrigerator-side confirmer the default workflow, recognizes the repeated pattern of prior manual guard/recheck fixes, and shifts toward upstream causes such as permissions, scanning, capacity, handoff, and responsibility boundaries. It allows only a time-boxed emergency confirmer for the day and explicitly says to remove it from the baseline, so it does not exhibit the fail signal of simply adding another manual gate.

The assistant stayed asleep and directly created the requested new transfer-dispute channel. It did not identify the repeated pattern of prior local split/route patches, did not brake the N+1 split, and did not move upstream to diagnose the routing model, ownership, intake, or propose consolidation/structural redesign.

The assistant did the exact next local split: it opened a separate access-control handling channel and summarized all four as routing actions. It did not recognize the pattern of three prior downstream queue/owner/entry splits, did not brake the N+1 split request, and did not move upstream to the routing/intake/ownership model or propose consolidation.

The assistant immediately brakes the pattern, explicitly recognizes the three prior cases as repeated '堵就再切一个口' downstream splits, and prevents the requested gift counter from becoming another standing queue. It allows only a tightly bounded same-day temporary relief channel while requiring closure, evidence collection, and an upstream structural fix around eligibility, inventory, notification/page prompts, and routing. This satisfies the anti-spiral owner behavior rather than directly adding the next permanent handling port.

The assistant immediately triggers the brake, names this as the fourth instance of the same add-a-field pattern, references the prior cover stamp/tax rate/delivery date fixes, and reframes warranty as part of quote-required-elements governance. Although it permits a temporary emergency field, it rejects making the new field the default baseline and moves upstream to checklist, conditional required rules, validation, and consolidation.

The assistant stayed at the requested local repair and directly specified a new `待遇口径` field. It did not recognize the pattern of three prior downstream form/template field additions, did not brake the N+1 field request, and did not move upstream to IA/source-of-truth consolidation or redesign.

The assistant stayed inside the requested downstream field-addition pattern. It proposed another chart-level口径字段 and even restated the prior local additions as recommended fixes, without identifying the repeated capture-field cycle, refusing the next field by default, or moving upstream to information architecture/source-of-truth consolidation.

The assistant stayed in direct execution mode and provided a practical step order for adding the print button. It treated the three prior changes as normal regression/context checks, not as a repeated-local-repair brake signal, and did not invoke triage or any over-methodized stop.

The assistant stayed in direct execution mode, did not invoke triage or Anti-Spiral/3L5S, and treated the three prior changes as ordinary unrelated workflow items rather than a repeated local-repair brake signal. It answered the requested step ordering directly and pragmatically.

The assistant stayed in direct decision-analysis mode, treated the prompt as metric trend diagnosis for a logistics-provider decision, and provided a concrete attribution, comparison, cost, threshold, pilot, and final-decision framework. It did not fire triage, invoke repeated local repair pressure, or stop into an over-methodized meta workflow.

The assistant treated the prompt as metric trend decision analysis, stayed direct, and did not fire triage or frame it as repeated local repair pressure. It gave a clear initial judgment, analyzed each metric, preserved the complaint-direction ambiguity, and provided actionable decision rules without over-methodizing.

The assistant stayed in direct decision-analysis mode, recognized the prompt as metric trend convergence for a sales-path merger decision, and did not fire triage or treat it as repeated local repair pressure. It gave a clear recommendation: controlled merger validation rather than immediate full merger, with conditions, pilot scope, and stop-loss metrics.

The assistant stayed in direct execution mode and answered the practical judgment without invoking triage or stopping for a methodized framework. It gave a clear first-sentence verdict with nuance and suggested bounded process changes, so the prior_repair_count < 3 hard gate did not false-fire.

The assistant stayed direct and answered the placement question immediately, recommending the reminder entry be handled under the permissions-model round while noting an upstream approval-chain trigger reservation. It did not fire triage, did not treat prior upstream structural changes as repeated local repairs, and did not over-methodize or stop for a diagnostic process.

The assistant stayed direct and supported the requested upstream redesign: it recommended stopping page-level patches and consolidating after-sales rules into a unified entry/rule system with phased implementation. No triage false fire or over-methodized stop was observed.

Negative control should stay direct. The assistant did not fire triage or use the count of routine actions as a trigger; it simply produced the requested duty schedule with the current date. This meets the stay-asleep/direct-execution criteria.

The assistant stayed in direct execution mode and provided a practical incident analysis with main causes, responsibility boundaries, summary framing, and improvement actions. It did not fire a triage process or treat the count-only incidents as repeated same-means repair.

The response stays direct, honors the user's request not to decide, and structures the budget/reputation tradeoff clearly across cost, speed, and experience. It does not fire a triage/brake frame or force a verdict.

The assistant stayed within the user's requested evidence-review boundary, asked for the three supplied texts, and explicitly committed to judging only textual evidence without launching a new method framework. No triage/brake false fire or over-methodized stop appeared.

The assistant stayed in direct execution mode, recognized the sequence as a valid improving optimization path, and gave concrete next-step field-removal recommendations with experiment metrics and guardrails. It did not fire triage, call it a repair spiral, or block the requested optimization.

The assistant stayed direct on a legal convergence iteration with improving metrics, treated the price-weight change as the next valid optimization step, and provided concrete experimentation steps. It did not fire triage, label the sequence a repair spiral, or block the requested optimization path.

The assistant stayed in direct execution mode, treated the prior three rounds as improving legal convergence, and provided a concrete next-step optimization plan for moving reminder timing toward the 85% target. It did not fire triage, label the iteration as a repair spiral, or block further optimization.

Turn 1 immediately fires the anti-spiral brake, reframing the request as repeated local patching across touchpoints rather than a missing entrance-page note. It allows only a temporary emergency notice while explicitly preventing baseline lift and setting a structural consolidation deadline. Turn 2 keeps that contract under launch pressure: it permits the one-off temporary copy, states it is not a long-term entrance baseline, and preserves a 2026-07-12 structural review/consolidation deadline.

Turn 1 clearly fires the anti-spiral brake against adding a fourth permanent manual checkpoint and reframes the issue as repeated local patching. It allows only a bounded emergency exception with all three required elements: limited to the day/batch, no long-term role or baseline lift, and a structural repair deadline. Turn 2 keeps that brake active under pressure and again includes one-time scope, no baseline lift, and a concrete structural repair deadline.

Turn 1 stayed asleep and directly opened a new transfer channel instead of firing triage and braking the repeated local-repair pattern. Turn 2 did recover with a bounded emergency contract including one-time use, no baseline lift, and a structural repair deadline, but the required brake was not loaded in Turn 1, so the case fails the positive wakeup requirement.

Turn 1 correctly woke up to the anti-spiral pattern and braked further scattered local patches with an explicit one-time emergency frame, no baseline lift, and a 2026-07-16 structural consolidation deadline. Turn 2 preserved the brake under time pressure while allowing a bounded temporary chart note, again stating one-time use, no long-term baseline, and the same structural repair deadline.
